verses.text: The generations of Aaron and Moses. Sons of Aaron: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, Ithamar — anointed priests. Nadab and Abihu died before the LORD when they offered strange fire; they had no children. Eleazar and Ithamar ministered before their father. Guidance: The opening verse pairs Aaron and Moses as the family of priests and prophets. But the very next line is grief: two sons gone. The priestly line carries a wound the rest of the book can't forget.

The LORD: bring Levi near; present them before Aaron. Keep his charge, the charge of the congregation before the tabernacle. Keep the instruments, do the service. Give the Levites wholly to Aaron. Aaron and sons are appointed priests; the stranger that comes nigh — death. Guidance: A whole tribe given to one family. Levi serves; Aaron alone offers. God draws a firm line between minister and priest — every helper does sacred work, but only the called few approach the altar.

The LORD: I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn of Israel; the Levites shall be Mine. For all the firstborn are Mine; on the day I smote Egypt's firstborn I sanctified Israel's firstborn — man and beast. Guidance: The night of Passover, every firstborn of Israel was claimed by God. Now the Levites stand in for them tribe-wide. Substitution again — God always counts His own, but lets someone stand in their place.

Number the sons of Levi by fathers' house. Every male from a month old upward. Sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Gershon: Libni, Shimei. Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, Uzziel. Merari: Mahli, Mushi. These are the families of the Levites. Guidance: Three branches of Levi — Gershon, Kohath, Merari. Each will get a side of the tabernacle and a piece of the load. The smallest tribe by the count, but the closest to God's house.

Of Gershon — the Libnites and Shimites. Numbered: 7,500 males. They encamp behind the tabernacle westward. Eliasaph son of Lael, chief. Their charge: the tabernacle, tent, covering, door hanging, court hangings, court gate, cords. Guidance: Gershon carries the fabric — curtains, coverings, ropes. The big soft pieces that make God's house feel like a home. Not the gold; the wool and linen. Hidden grace runs through the threads.

Of Kohath — Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, Uzzielites. Numbered: 8,600 males keeping the sanctuary. Encamp south side. Elizaphan son of Uzziel, chief. Charge: the ark, table, candlestick, altars, the vail. Eleazar son of Aaron — chief over the Levite chiefs. Guidance: Kohath carries the holy furniture — ark, table, lampstand, altars. The most dangerous job; one wrong touch could kill. Moses and Aaron's family is from Kohath. Burden and privilege braided.

Of Merari — the Mahlites and Mushites. Numbered: 6,200 males. Encamp north side. Zuriel son of Abihail, chief. Charge: the boards, bars, pillars, sockets, vessels, the pillars of the court, sockets, pins, cords. Guidance: Merari carries the heavy stuff — boards, pillars, sockets, bars. The frame on which the holy hangs. Not the eye-catching part, but without it, nothing stands. Some servants hold up the whole house unnoticed.

Moses, Aaron, and his sons encamp before the tabernacle eastward, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the children of Israel; the stranger that comes nigh shall die. All the Levites numbered: 22,000 males a month old upward. Guidance: Moses and Aaron camp at the front door — the gatekeepers of God's house. The total Levites — 22,000 — is the smallest tribal count by far. The smallest cluster carries the holiest weight.

Moses, Aaron, and his sons encamp before the tabernacle eastward, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the children of Israel; the stranger that comes nigh shall die. All the Levites numbered: 22,000 males a month old upward. Guidance: Moses and Aaron camp at the front door — the gatekeepers of God's house. The total Levites — 22,000 — is the smallest tribal count by far. The smallest cluster carries the holiest weight.

The LORD: number the firstborn of Israel a month old upward. Take Levites for Me instead; Levites' cattle for firstborn cattle. Numbered: 22,273 firstborn males. 273 more than Levites — redeem at 5 shekels apiece; give the money to Aaron. Moses took 1,365 shekels and gave to Aaron. Guidance: 273 firstborn had no Levite to stand in for them — so each pays 5 shekels and is bought back. God counts every soul. When the substitute runs short, ransom money covers the gap.
